name: (V2) Billie & Louise | Your Sugar Mommy and her Sugar Mommy
description:
New definition, same intro.

Original here: [First Edition of Billie and Louise]

Update on 2026/Jan/3rd 07:28:24: Added NEW intro (in intro 3); the first original bot was also updated too. Someone asked for poly alt. Well... they'd said maybe an ALT for "happy poly," but, uh, logic beat me. After the first line, everything just flowed out and became what you read. My apolocheese, you will need the apology from me after you finish reading the new intro... I guess. Hey, it's still poly.


I won't write much info about them in the bot description—I'm lazy af (also this was not really a new bot). Instead, here's a short Q&A.

Q: Why a new bot instead of updating the old one?

A: Because the old version wasn't bad. Some pookies already got used to it and found it works well. But everyone RPs under different conditions: custom prompts, LLM models (including server hiccups, resource allocation, etc.), personal reply style. A new version might work better for some, worse for others. Now you can choose.

Q: Is this an upgrade? How different is it?

A: Not an upgrade—a variant. The definition structure is completely rewritten with different architecture and focus points. This version also uses a different lorebook with new triggering rules and expanded lore for both characters. Same girls, different engine under the hood. The original isn't broken—this is just built differently. Try both, keep whichever fits your vibe.

Q: Can I migrate my chat history?

A: Surely. If you're having trouble, find summarizing templates on JAI Reddit or the official JAI Discord—or ask me in the comments. After summarizing, paste it into "chat memory." I recommend also copying the last 4 messages of your old chat (Yours / Char / Yours / Char) into the new one.

Q: Will they feel weird even if I summarize properly?

A: Probably. The old and new definitions are written differently, with different focus points. Keep the old one, try the new one, go back if the new one feels meh.

Q: Two intros?

A: Same thing, only the second intro with * for actions, for different reading preferences.

⚠️ About Character Development

Character development is intentionally slow. These characters protect themselves—trust and change take time. If you want faster progress, push harder in your RP, but expect resistance. That resistance is part of who they are.<
creator: JunJuneJunie
